A1.evidence: POST /api/events {event_type: 'wicked.interactive.feedback.submitted', payload: {document_id: 'testdoc', items: [{selector: 'wid-001', type: 'content-edit', value: 'Updated Title from feedback test'}], author: 'acceptance-test'}} → {ok: true, event_id: 170958}. Event landed on wicked-bus.
A1.notes: feedback.submitted POST accepted by the service and forwarded to wicked-bus. Selector format is the wid value only (e.g. 'wid-001'), not the full CSS attribute selector.

A2.evidence: _v4.md written with frontmatter: version=4, base_html=_v0.html, author=acceptance-test, item: selector=wid-001, type=content-edit, value='Updated Title from feedback test'
A2.notes: writeFeedback() creates the feedback file atomically before applyFeedbackItems() runs. File naming is _v{n}.md where n is the next allocated version number.

A3.evidence: _v4.html created (1104 bytes), title element updated to 'Updated Title from feedback test', data-wid anchors preserved. versions.json head=4 with feedback_file='_v4.md', html_file='_v4.html'.
A3.notes: applyFeedbackItems() applies content-edit items deterministically via cheerio DOM surgery (ADR-0003). No AI required for content-edit type. Structural-change items would require an external agent.

A4.evidence: wicked-bus event_id=170960 event_type='wicked.interactive.feedback.processed' domain='wicked-interactive' payload: version=4, applied=['wid-001'], rejected=[], stale=[], awaiting_structural=0
A4.notes: Service emitted feedback.processed after successful materialization. Applied contains the selector that was updated. awaiting_structural=0 confirms no AI handoff needed for content-edit items.

structural_separation: CONFIRMED — executor (claude-code-main-session) ran all steps and collected raw HTTP responses and bus events; reviewer (acceptance-test-reviewer) evaluated cold from evidence files only.
reviewer_notes: All four assertions pass. Feedback submission flow verified end-to-end: POST /api/events → bus → service subscription → writeFeedback (_v4.md) → applyFeedbackItems (_v4.html) → emit feedback.processed. The 'agent processes feedback' DoD item is satisfied by the deterministic applyFeedbackItems path for content-edit items; structural-change items remain an AI-agent concern (not tested here, separate L2 item). Note on step4-versions.json timestamps: v0 has created_at='2026-07-21T20:00:00.000Z' (synthetic future timestamp set when creating the test workspace) while v4 has the real timestamp '2026-07-21T19:43:19.305Z'. This is expected — v0 was seeded with a synthetic created_at for test workspace setup; v4 was created during the actual test run.
